label: T=0 — Admission
narrative: The patient arrives to the ED with worsening right foot pain, purulent drainage, and visible bone at the wound base. Blood cultures have been drawn; results are pending.

label: T=12 Hours — Imaging & Cultures
narrative: Overnight data return. MRI confirms osteomyelitis of the 5th metatarsal head with an adjacent soft tissue abscess. Blood cultures are positive.

new_information.content: MRI right foot: osteomyelitis of 5th metatarsal head with adjacent soft tissue abscess confirmed.
Blood cultures ×2: Gram-positive cocci in clusters — identification pending.
SCr now 2.3 (up from 1.9 on admission). Estimated CrCl now approximately 22 mL/min.

label: T=24 Hours — Source Control
narrative: The patient remains febrile with persistent purulent drainage. Podiatry is available for surgical consultation. Source control is a critical next step for deep infection with bacteremia.

new_information.content: Podiatry available for urgent debridement. Abscess and osteomyelitis require source control.

label: T=36 Hours — Renal Dosing
narrative: Renal function has worsened on empiric therapy. SCr is 2.3, estimated CrCl approximately 22 mL/min. Review your current regimen and adjust dosing as needed.

new_information.content: SCr 2.3 mg/dL. CrCl ~22 mL/min. Review renally cleared agents.

label: T=48 Hours — Culture Reveal
narrative: Blood culture results have finalized. The organism has been identified and susceptibilities are available.

label: T=5–7 Days — Clinical Response
narrative: Repeat blood cultures and wound assessment are available. The patient's clinical trajectory depends on source control, definitive therapy, and monitoring.

new_information.content: Repeat blood cultures at 72 hours — results per clinical course.
Surgical debridement status and bone biopsy results on chart.
SCr trending per renal course. WBC and fever trend available.

label: T=7–10 Days — Duration & Route
narrative: With source control and culture data in hand, plan the duration and route of therapy for MSSA osteomyelitis with bacteremia.

label: Discharge Planning
narrative: Outpatient antimicrobial course is being planned. Build the monitoring and OPAT structure for safe transition.
